papers.title: The Swedish Heart Failure Registry biobank: rationale, design, and initial findings
papers.institution: Karolinska Institutet / Swedish Heart Failure Registry
papers.access_route: Swedish HF Registry biobank committee application; linked to SwedeHF national registry
papers.notes: Cardiac tissue paired with plasma and omics layers; comorbidity burden (including atherosclerosis) documented via national registry linkage; longitudinal follow-up with mortality endpoints

papers.title: SWEDEHEART: a national quality registry and research platform for cardiovascular disease
papers.access_route: SWEDEHEART data access committee; Uppsala Biobank for specimens; national registry linkage standard
papers.notes: EDTA plasma at -80C; large national registry with atherosclerosis and CHF subsets; detailed medication and comorbidity annotation; specimen availability depends on subset enrollment

papers.title: Endomyocardial biopsy-derived transcriptomic signatures identify subtypes of dilated cardiomyopathy
papers.access_route: Collaborative research agreement with JHU cardiology; IRB approval required
papers.notes: Matched endomyocardial biopsy + plasma from 88 HF patients; atherosclerosis comorbidity documented; RNA extraction from biopsy confirmed; small n limits statistical power but high tissue quality

papers.title: Human heart biobanks: the cornerstone of basic and translational cardiac research
papers.journal: Journal of Molecular and Cellular Cardiology
papers.access_route: Sydney Heart Bank: sydneyheartbank.org.au; BMCB: collaborative agreement with UBC
papers.notes: Review describing two major cardiac biobanks; Sydney Heart Bank has >2700 cardiac samples; BMCB (British Columbia Molecular Biomarker Consortium) plasma + tissue matched; both accessible via application

expected_retrievable_institutions: Karolinska Institutet / Swedish Heart Failure Registry
notes: Three European registry-linked biobanks with plasma; JHU provides the only matched endomyocardial biopsy + plasma collection with n<100 but high depth. Sydney Heart Bank / BMCB are accessible via application and provide cardiac tissue + plasma.
